title: ds-viz-table - option matrix
description: Test bench for splunk.table. Walks every documented option group: chrome (count, font, fontSize, headerVisibility, showRowNumbers, showInternalFields, backgroundColor), tableFormat (align, cellTypes incl. SparklineCell, rowBackgroundColors, rowColors, headerBackgroundColor, headerColor, sparklineColors, sparklineAreaColors, sparklineTypes), and columnFormat (per-column align, cellTypes, width, rowBackgroundColors, rowColors, sparklineColors). Includes heatmap row-shading via _color_rank rank field + seriesByName + rangeValue.

options.query: | makeresults count=5 | streamstats count AS rn | eval host = case(rn==1,"web-01",rn==2,"web-02",rn==3,"db-01",rn==4,"app-03",1==1,"cache-02") | eval severity = case(rn==1,"critical",rn==2,"high",rn==3,"medium",rn==4,"low",1==1,"info") | eval cpu = case(rn==1,94,rn==2,82,rn==3,71,rn==4,55,1==1,33) | eval message = case(rn==1,"Disk full",rn==2,"High latency",rn==3,"Slow query",rn==4,"Job retry",1==1,"Healthy") | table host severity cpu message

| makeresults count=5 | streamstats count AS rn | eval host = "web-" . tostring(rn) | eval status = case(rn<=2,"ok",1==1,"degraded") | eval _internal_id = "id-" . tostring(rn*100) | table _time host status _internal_id

ds_heatmap.name: heatmap - severity rank for row tinting

options.query: | makeresults count=5 | streamstats count AS rn | eval service = case(rn==1,"checkout",rn==2,"login",rn==3,"search",rn==4,"recs",1==1,"static") | eval severity = case(rn==1,"critical",rn==2,"high",rn==3,"medium",rn==4,"low",1==1,"info") | eval errors_per_min = case(rn==1,142,rn==2,67,rn==3,28,rn==4,9,1==1,1) | eval _color_rank = case(severity=="critical",1,severity=="high",2,severity=="medium",3,severity=="low",4,1==1,5) | table service severity errors_per_min _color_rank

ds_sparkline.name: sparkline - hosts with cpu mem req err trends

options.query: | makeresults count=288 | streamstats count AS rn | eval _time = relative_time(now(), "-" . tostring((288-rn)*5) . "m") | eval host = case((rn-1) % 4 == 0, "web-01", (rn-1) % 4 == 1, "web-02", (rn-1) % 4 == 2, "db-01", 1==1, "app-03") | eval base_cpu = case(host=="web-01",75,host=="web-02",55,host=="db-01",45,1==1,28) | eval base_mem = case(host=="web-01",68,host=="web-02",50,host=="db-01",72,1==1,32) | eval base_req = case(host=="web-01",1400,host=="web-02",640,host=="db-01",240,1==1,80) | eval base_err = case(host=="web-01",18,host=="web-02",4,host=="db-01",2,1==1,1) | eval cpu = base_cpu + (rn % 25) - 12 | eval mem = base_mem + (rn % 18) - 8 | eval req = base_req + (rn % 80) * 4 - 160 | eval err = base_err + (rn % 7) - 3 | stats latest(cpu) AS current_cpu, sparkline(avg(cpu), 30m) AS trend_cpu, sparkline(avg(mem), 30m) AS trend_mem, sparkline(sum(req), 30m) AS trend_req, sparkline(sum(err), 30m) AS trend_err by host

ds_align.name: align - mixed types for alignment demo

options.query: | makeresults count=4 | streamstats count AS rn | eval product = case(rn==1,"Headphones",rn==2,"Speaker",rn==3,"Mic",1==1,"Cable") | eval orders = case(rn==1,1284,rn==2,672,rn==3,341,1==1,89) | eval revenue_usd = case(rn==1,38520.50,rn==2,16800.00,rn==3,8525.25,1==1,1335.10) | eval status = case(rn==1,"OK",rn==2,"OK",rn==3,"WARN",1==1,"FAIL") | table product orders revenue_usd status

options.markdown:
# `splunk.table` - option matrix

The go-to viz for showing rows and columns. Comes with three layered formatting tiers:

1. **Global chrome** in `options` (font, headers, count, backgroundColor)
2. **`tableFormat`** for whole-table effects (align grid, cell renderers, row tinting, header colours, sparklines)
3. **`columnFormat`** for per-column overrides (width, align, cell type, rowBackgroundColors of one column)

Dynamic options live in `context` and are referenced via DOS expressions: `> table | seriesByName('rank_field') | rangeValue(bands)`.

**Always set `showInternalFields: false`** to hide rank fields like `_color_rank` from the rendered output.

viz_default.title: 1. Default - all 5 rows, header inline, internal fields shown
viz_default.description: Pure default. count=10 (we have 5 rows so all show), headerVisibility=inline, showRowNumbers=false, showInternalFields=true. Columns auto-aligned by type.

viz_count_pagination.title: 2. count: 3 + headerVisibility: fixed + showRowNumbers: true
viz_count_pagination.description: count caps the rendered rows (paginator handles the rest). headerVisibility=fixed pins the header so it stays put when scrolling. showRowNumbers prepends a row index.

viz_internal_hidden.title: 3. showInternalFields: false (hides _internal_id, but _time stays)
viz_internal_hidden.description: Doc note: 'The _time field will still be shown if false.' Underscore-prefixed user fields like `_internal_id` and our `_color_rank` rank field are hidden, but `_time` is exempt. ALWAYS set this to false when you use a `_color_rank` pattern.

viz_font_mono.title: 4. font: monospace + fontSize: small
viz_font_mono.description: Monospace for log/payload tables where columns line up vertically. fontSize: extraSmall (10px), small (12px), default (14px), large (16px).

viz_align_columnformat.title: 5. columnFormat: per-column align + width
viz_align_columnformat.description: Set alignment and width per column. Numbers right-align, status center, text left. width is in pixels. The `align` value here is a single string (the column applies it to every cell) - the GLOBAL `tableFormat.align` is a 2D array.

viz_header_colors.title: 6. tableFormat.headerBackgroundColor + headerColor
viz_header_colors.description: Header chrome via tableFormat. Global static colours - the value is a literal hex, not a DOS expression. Use this to match the panel chrome to a brand colour or to deemphasise the header.

viz_heatmap.title: 7. Heatmap rows via _color_rank + rangeValue (severity tinting)
viz_heatmap.description: The classic pattern. Compute a numeric rank field upstream (`_color_rank`), keep `showInternalFields: false`, and feed the rank into `tableFormat.rowBackgroundColors` and `rowColors` via DOS. The bands in `context` map rank to colour. Notice critical row reads RED end-to-end without explicit row-by-row config. Header colours are intentionally NOT overridden here so they fall back to `themes.defaultHeaderBackgroundColor` and stay readable in both dark and light theme.

viz_sparkline.title: 8. SparklineCell - canonical `stats sparkline()` pattern
viz_sparkline.description: Inline sparklines per row. Two things must be right at once: (1) the SPL must produce REAL multivalue fields with enough datapoints across the dashboard's time window. `| makeresults count=288 | eval _time = relative_time(now(), "-" . tostring((288-rn)*5) . "m") | ... | stats latest(...) AS current_x, sparkline(avg(x), 30m) AS trend_x by host` gives 51 datapoints per host across `-24h@h, now`. With too few datapoints (or all-zero buckets), Splunk falls back to a flat default colour and only row 1 looks right. (2) `columnFormat.<col>.sparklineColors` and `sparklineTypes` are `string[]` where each element styles ONE row of that column - if you have 4 rows you pass 4 entries. Splunk auto-detects multivalue columns as SparklineCell via the default `tableFormat.cellTypes` DOS, so no need to set `cellTypes` per column. Anti-pattern: faking mv with `eval x="a,b,c" | makemv delim/tokenizer x` produces strings, not multivalue fields.

viz_count_one.title: 9. count: 1 + headerVisibility: none
viz_count_one.description: Stripped down. headerVisibility=none kills the header row entirely - useful for KPI mini-tables embedded in a grid where the title carries the meaning.

viz_bg_color.title: 10. backgroundColor: panel tint
viz_bg_color.description: Global backgroundColor at the table level. Different from the panel chrome - this paints the cells. Pair with header colours so the gradient reads as one unit.

viz_compact_large.title: 11. fontSize: large + headerVisibility: fixed
viz_compact_large.description: Bigger font (16px) for an executive readout. Combined with fixed header for scroll friendliness even though count<rows.

viz_align_grid.title: 12. tableFormat.align via DOS - auto by type
viz_align_grid.description: Default global align: `> table | type() | matchValue(tableAlignByType)`. Numbers go right, strings left, mixed center. For full control, override per-column via columnFormat.align (1D array) or the global tableFormat.align (2D array).
